decisions.decision: Source URLs restricted to aggregation indexes only — no politician press-release slugs
decisions.rationale: AI research agents fabricate plausible press-release URL slugs from knowledge of the politician record + URL pattern. They look real but are hallucinated. Discovered when checking Spanberger migration post-execution.

decisions.decision: OnTheIssues path for Virginia politicians: use /VA/ not /House/
decisions.rationale: /House/Abigail_Spanberger.htm returns 404. /VA/Abigail_Spanberger.htm verified live. Apply same logic for other VA officials who have left their prior chamber role.

decisions.decision: VPAP candidate IDs unverified — omit from future plans
decisions.rationale: VPAP returned 403, couldn't confirm ID 75011 is Spanberger. Removed from remaining plans pending manual browser verification.

next_action: Spawn Wave 2 executor for Plan 106-02 (Hashmi). Pass source restriction constraint: aggregation indexes only (ballotpedia.org, ontheissues.org with correct path, congress.gov for bill numbers, real news with date-based paths). No politician.gov/press-releases/ slugs.
context_notes: Plan 01 stance values and reasoning are accurate — AI correctly knew Spanberger's congressional record. The problem was specifically with citation URLs: press-release slugs are pattern-generated, not fetched. The correction is clean. Plans 02-05 (state/federal officials) should yield rich records. Plans 06-07 (Alexandria council/ACPS) have D-03/D-04 5-minute sliding cap — expect fewer stances per person.
